test_name: Verify successful user login with valid credentials
test_description: Validate that a registered Moogsoft user can successfully authenticate into the platform using valid username and password and is redirected to the default dashboard view as defined in the BRD.
preconditions: User account exists in Moogsoft with active status and assigned role.
Expected Result: User is authenticated, session token is issued, and the Situation Room/Dashboard is displayed.
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the Moogsoft login URL
Step 2: Enter a valid username
Step 3: Enter the corresponding valid password
Step 4: Click the Login button
Step 5: Verify redirection to the default landing dashboard

test_name: Verify login failure with invalid password
test_description: Ensure that the system rejects authentication attempts with a valid username but invalid password and displays an appropriate error message without exposing sensitive information.
preconditions: Valid user exists in the system.
Expected Result: Login is rejected with a generic error message such as 'Invalid username or password'. No session is created.
steps: Step 1: Open login page
Step 2: Enter valid username
Step 3: Enter an incorrect password
Step 4: Click Login
Step 5: Verify generic authentication error is displayed

test_name: Verify account lockout after consecutive failed login attempts
test_description: Validate that after the configured threshold of failed login attempts, the user account is temporarily locked to mitigate brute-force attacks.
preconditions: Account lockout policy is configured (e.g., 5 attempts).
Expected Result: After exceeding the threshold, the account is locked and subsequent attempts display a lockout message.
steps: Step 1: Attempt login with wrong password N times consecutively
Step 2: On the N+1 attempt, observe the response
Step 3: Verify account lockout message
Step 4: Verify even correct password is rejected until unlock

test_name: Verify SSO/SAML authentication integration
test_description: Validate that users configured for SSO can authenticate through the configured Identity Provider and are provisioned into Moogsoft with correct role mapping.
preconditions: SSO/SAML IdP is configured and user exists in IdP.
Expected Result: User is redirected to IdP, authenticated, redirected back, and granted role-appropriate access.
steps: Step 1: Access Moogsoft login URL
Step 2: Select SSO login option
Step 3: Authenticate via IdP
Step 4: Verify SAML assertion is consumed
Step 5: Verify role and permissions match IdP attributes

test_name: Verify event ingestion via REST API LAM
test_description: Validate that events posted via the REST LAM endpoint are accepted, parsed, and converted into Moogsoft events visible in the Open Events view.
preconditions: REST LAM is configured, running, and authentication token is available.
Expected Result: HTTP 200 response returned and event appears in Open Events with correctly mapped fields (source, severity, description, manager).
steps: Step 1: Construct a valid event payload in JSON
Step 2: POST payload to the REST LAM endpoint with auth header
Step 3: Verify HTTP 200 success response
Step 4: Open Events view and search for ingested event
Step 5: Validate field mappings

test_name: Verify event ingestion rejects malformed payload
test_description: Ensure that the LAM returns an appropriate error and does not create an event when the payload is malformed or missing required fields.
preconditions: REST LAM is running.
Expected Result: HTTP 400 response with descriptive error; no event created in Moogsoft.
steps: Step 1: Send POST request with invalid JSON
Step 2: Verify 400 Bad Request response
Step 3: Send valid JSON missing required field 'signature'
Step 4: Verify rejection with field-level error
Step 5: Confirm no event exists in Open Events

test_name: Verify event deduplication via signature
test_description: Validate that incoming events sharing the same signature are deduplicated and the existing event's count is incremented instead of creating duplicates.
preconditions: An event with signature S exists in Open Events.
Expected Result: Subsequent events with signature S do not create new event rows; the original event's count/last-occurrence timestamp updates.
steps: Step 1: Send event with signature S
Step 2: Send identical event again
Step 3: Open Open Events view
Step 4: Verify only one row exists for signature S
Step 5: Verify count incremented and last-occurrence updated

test_name: Verify alert clustering into Situations by algorithm
test_description: Validate that correlated alerts are clustered into a Situation by the configured clustering algorithm (e.g., Cookbook, Tempus, Classic).
preconditions: Clustering algorithm is configured; multiple correlated alerts ingested within the time window.
Expected Result: A Situation is created containing the correlated alerts with correct description, severity, and assigned moderator.
steps: Step 1: Ingest a set of correlated alerts
Step 2: Wait for the clustering interval
Step 3: Open Situation Room
Step 4: Verify Situation is created with expected alerts
Step 5: Validate severity is highest of contained alerts

test_name: Verify Situation severity escalation when higher severity alert added
test_description: Ensure a Situation's severity escalates automatically when a newly clustered alert has higher severity than current Situation severity.
preconditions: Open Situation exists with severity 'Minor'.
Expected Result: Situation severity updates to 'Critical' when a Critical alert joins the cluster.
steps: Step 1: Identify open Situation with Minor severity
Step 2: Ingest a correlated Critical alert
Step 3: Verify alert is added to Situation
Step 4: Verify Situation severity updates to Critical
Step 5: Verify audit log records the change

test_name: Verify Situation assignment to moderator/team
test_description: Validate that Situations can be assigned to a moderator or team and ownership is reflected across UI and notifications.
preconditions: Open Situation exists; user has assignment permission.
Expected Result: Situation owner is updated, notifications are sent, and assignment is visible in audit log.
steps: Step 1: Open a Situation
Step 2: Click Assign and select moderator
Step 3: Confirm assignment
Step 4: Verify owner displayed in Situation header
Step 5: Verify assignee receives notification

test_name: Verify Situation closure workflow
test_description: Validate the full lifecycle closure of a Situation including resolution notes, status update, and downstream notifications.
preconditions: An open Situation is assigned to the current user.
Expected Result: Situation transitions to Closed/Resolved status, resolution is captured, and contained alerts are also resolved per policy.
steps: Step 1: Open assigned Situation
Step 2: Click Resolve
Step 3: Enter resolution notes
Step 4: Submit closure
Step 5: Verify status, audit trail, and downstream ticket update

test_name: Verify role-based access control for Operator role
test_description: Validate that users with Operator role can view and acknowledge Situations but cannot perform admin functions such as user management or integration configuration.
preconditions: Operator user exists.
Expected Result: Operator-allowed actions succeed; restricted admin actions are hidden or return authorization errors.
steps: Step 1: Login as Operator
Step 2: Acknowledge a Situation
Step 3: Attempt to access user management page
Step 4: Verify access denied
Step 5: Attempt to modify integration; verify denial

test_name: Verify ServiceNow integration creates incident from Situation
test_description: Validate the bi-directional ServiceNow integration creates a corresponding incident when a Situation is escalated and links its number back to Moogsoft.
preconditions: ServiceNow integration is configured with valid credentials and instance URL.
Expected Result: Incident is created in ServiceNow with mapped fields; SN incident number appears on the Moogsoft Situation.
steps: Step 1: Open a Situation
Step 2: Click 'Create ServiceNow Incident'
Step 3: Verify incident appears in ServiceNow
Step 4: Verify SN incident number recorded on Situation
Step 5: Update SN incident and verify status sync back to Moogsoft

test_name: Verify email notification sent on Situation creation
test_description: Ensure email notification is dispatched to configured recipients when a new Situation is created based on notification policy.
preconditions: Email integration configured; notification policy defined.
Expected Result: Email is delivered with Situation summary, severity, ID, and deep link.
steps: Step 1: Trigger creation of a new Situation
Step 2: Wait for notification trigger
Step 3: Check recipient inbox
Step 4: Validate email content and link

test_name: Verify maintenance window suppresses alerts
test_description: Validate that alerts received during a configured maintenance window for a host/service are suppressed and not promoted to Situations.
preconditions: Maintenance window scheduled for host H covering current time.
Expected Result: Alerts for host H are suppressed with status 'maintenance' and do not generate Situations.
steps: Step 1: Configure maintenance window for host H
Step 2: Ingest alert for host H during window
Step 3: Verify alert is suppressed
Step 4: Verify no Situation is created
Step 5: End window and verify subsequent alerts process normally

test_name: Verify enrichment rules add CMDB data to alerts
test_description: Validate that enrichment rules pulling data from CMDB add owner, location, and business service attributes to incoming alerts.
preconditions: CMDB integration configured; enrichment rule active.
Expected Result: Ingested alert contains enriched fields populated from CMDB lookup.
steps: Step 1: Ingest alert for CI present in CMDB
Step 2: Open alert details
Step 3: Verify enriched fields populated
Step 4: Ingest alert for unknown CI
Step 5: Verify enrichment gracefully skipped

test_name: Verify maximum event payload size handling
test_description: Validate behavior when payload exceeds configured maximum size limit at LAM ingestion.
preconditions: Configured max payload size known.
Expected Result: Oversized payload is rejected with HTTP 413; system remains stable; valid sized payload immediately after is processed.
steps: Step 1: Construct payload exceeding limit
Step 2: POST to LAM
Step 3: Verify 413 response
Step 4: Send valid payload
Step 5: Verify successful processing

test_name: Verify high-volume event ingestion throughput
test_description: Validate the system sustains the documented events-per-second throughput without dropping events or degrading UI responsiveness.
preconditions: Performance test environment provisioned per BRD sizing.
Expected Result: All ingested events accounted for; latency within SLO; no LAM crashes.
steps: Step 1: Start event generator at target EPS
Step 2: Run sustained load for 30 minutes
Step 3: Monitor LAM and processor metrics
Step 4: Reconcile sent vs. received counts
Step 5: Verify Situation Room remains responsive

test_name: Verify field validation for required event attributes
test_description: Ensure events missing mandatory attributes (signature, source, severity) are rejected with field-specific errors.
preconditions: LAM running.
Expected Result: Each missing required field generates a clear validation error and the event is not stored.
steps: Step 1: Send payload missing 'signature'
Step 2: Send payload missing 'severity'
Step 3: Send payload missing 'source'
Step 4: Verify each is rejected with proper error

test_name: Verify severity boundary values are accepted
test_description: Validate severities at boundary values (0=Clear and 5=Critical) are properly accepted and mapped.
preconditions: LAM accepting events.
Expected Result: Events with severity 0 and 5 are stored with correct severity labels; out-of-range severities (-1, 6) are rejected.
steps: Step 1: Send event with severity 0
Step 2: Send event with severity 5
Step 3: Send event with severity -1
Step 4: Send event with severity 6
Step 5: Verify acceptance/rejection accordingly

test_name: Verify Clear severity auto-resolves matching alerts
test_description: Validate that a Clear event auto-resolves matching active alerts based on signature.
preconditions: Active alert with signature S exists.
Expected Result: On receipt of Clear event matching signature S, the alert is resolved and any Situation reviewed for closure.
steps: Step 1: Confirm active alert with signature S
Step 2: Send Clear event for signature S
Step 3: Verify alert is resolved
Step 4: Verify Situation updated if all contained alerts cleared

test_name: Verify audit log captures user actions
test_description: Ensure all critical user actions (login, assignment, closure, configuration change) are written to the immutable audit log.
preconditions: Audit logging enabled.
Expected Result: Each action produces an audit entry with user, timestamp, action, and target object.
steps: Step 1: Perform login
Step 2: Assign and resolve a Situation
Step 3: Modify a configuration
Step 4: Query the audit log
Step 5: Verify entries exist with correct attribution

test_name: Verify session timeout after inactivity
test_description: Validate that user sessions expire after the configured inactivity period and re-authentication is required.
preconditions: Session timeout policy configured (e.g., 30 minutes).
Expected Result: After inactivity period, user is logged out and redirected to login.
steps: Step 1: Login and remain idle past timeout
Step 2: Attempt an action
Step 3: Verify redirection to login
Step 4: Verify re-authentication required

test_name: Verify API rate limiting enforcement
test_description: Validate that API rate limits return 429 when exceeded and reset after the window.
preconditions: Rate limit configured per token.
Expected Result: Requests beyond the limit return HTTP 429; subsequent requests succeed after window reset.
steps: Step 1: Send burst exceeding rate limit
Step 2: Verify 429 responses
Step 3: Wait for window reset
Step 4: Send request and verify success

test_name: Verify database failover continuity
test_description: Validate that the platform continues operating with minimal data loss during database primary failover.
preconditions: HA database cluster configured.
Expected Result: Ingestion continues with brief degradation; no committed events lost; UI recovers automatically.
steps: Step 1: Begin steady-state event ingestion
Step 2: Trigger DB primary failover
Step 3: Monitor ingestion and UI
Step 4: Reconcile pre/post counts
Step 5: Verify no committed data lost

test_name: Verify export of Situation report to CSV
test_description: Validate that users can export filtered Situation data to CSV with all visible columns.
preconditions: User has reporting permission; Situations exist.
Expected Result: CSV file downloads with correct rows matching filter and all selected columns.
steps: Step 1: Apply filter on Situations view
Step 2: Click Export to CSV
Step 3: Open the file
Step 4: Verify row count and column completeness

test_name: Verify SQL injection protection on search inputs
test_description: Ensure search and filter fields are protected against SQL injection attempts.
preconditions: User has access to search UI.
Expected Result: Injection payloads are sanitized/escaped; no errors, no unauthorized data exposure.
steps: Step 1: Enter SQL injection payload in search
Step 2: Submit search
Step 3: Verify safe handling and normal empty/result response
Step 4: Verify no DB error in logs

test_name: Verify XSS protection in alert/situation description fields
test_description: Ensure script payloads sent in event description are escaped when rendered in UI.
preconditions: User can ingest events.
Expected Result: Script tags are rendered as text, not executed; CSP headers present.
steps: Step 1: Ingest event with description containing <script>alert(1)</script>
Step 2: Open Open Events view
Step 3: Verify content rendered safely
Step 4: Verify no JS execution

test_name: Verify Slack/Teams collaboration channel auto-creation
test_description: Validate that when a Situation is created, the configured collaboration channel (Slack/Teams) is auto-provisioned and members are invited.
preconditions: Slack/Teams integration enabled; channel template configured.
Expected Result: Channel is created with naming convention, invited stakeholders, and initial Situation summary posted.
steps: Step 1: Trigger creation of new Situation
Step 2: Verify channel created in Slack/Teams
Step 3: Verify members invited
Step 4: Verify initial post contains Situation details and link

test_name: Verify retention policy purges aged data
test_description: Validate that events, alerts, and Situations older than configured retention period are purged or archived.
preconditions: Retention policy configured (e.g., 90 days).
Expected Result: Data older than retention threshold is removed/archived; UI and queries do not return purged records.
steps: Step 1: Identify records older than retention
Step 2: Trigger or wait for retention job
Step 3: Verify purge/archive completion
Step 4: Query for old records and verify absence

test_name: Open Questions for Clarification
test_description: The attached document 'Moogsoft BRD v4.1.3.docx' was referenced but its textual content was not accessible in this text-only mode. Test cases above are derived from standard Moogsoft AIOps platform capabilities and general BRD patterns. Confirm: (1) exact clustering algorithm and thresholds, (2) configured severity scale and field mappings, (3) RBAC matrix and role names, (4) enabled integrations (ServiceNow, Slack/Teams, email, SSO IdP), (5) retention/archival periods, (6) performance SLOs and target EPS, (7) maintenance window scope rules, (8) notification policy specifics, (9) audit log fields, (10) any custom enrichment/CMDB sources.

steps: Step 1: Trigger creation of new Situation

Step 3: Verify members invited
Step 4: Verify initial post contains Situation details and link

test_name: Verify retention policy purges aged data
test_description: Validate that events, alerts, and Situations older than configured retention period are purged or archived.
preconditions: Retention policy configured (e.g., 90 days).
Expected Result: Data older than retention threshold is removed/archived; UI and queries do not return purged records.
steps: Step 1: Identify records older than retention
Step 2: Trigger or wait for retention job
Step 3: Verify purge/archive completion
Step 4: Query for old records and verify absence

test_name: Open Questions for Clarification
test_description: The attached document 'Moogsoft BRD v4.1.3.docx' was referenced but its textual content was not accessible in this text-only mode. Test cases above are derived from standard Moogsoft AIOps platform capabilities and general BRD patterns. Confirm: (1) exact clustering algorithm and thresholds, (2) configured severity scale and field mappings, (3) RBAC matrix and role names, (4) enabled integrations (ServiceNow, Slack/Teams, email, SSO IdP), (5) retention/archival periods, (6) performance SLOs and target EPS, (7) maintenance window scope rules, (8) notification policy specifics, (9) audit log fields, (10) any custom enrichment/CMDB sources.
